checks.a_analysis: The conversion result follows the structure and content of the source document accurately for the sections provided. Titles like 'State of North Carolina – Broadband Infrastructure Office Title Slide' and table captions like 'Broadband Speeds by Activity' are AI-generated for accessibility/metadata, which are permitted. There is no evidence of hallucinated body text or invented paragraphs in the converted sections.

All contact information in the output, including URLs (it.nc.gov, fcc.gov), social media handles (@NCDIT, @BroadbandIO, @ncicenter), and the email address (firsttech@nc.gov), was verified against the source PDF. No mismatches or fabrications were found.

The source document contains 27 pages of information. The output JSON only contains content for pages 1, 2, 3, 4, 13, 15, 26, and 27. It explicitly contains a placeholder stating: '"_omitted": "18 sections omitted for token budget"'. This omission represents a massive loss of critical information from the source, including maps, policy details, and grant program data. In the context of document auditing, a partial conversion that omits the majority of the source content is a failure of fidelity.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains 27 distinct pages of content including 'North Carolina Broadband Service Inventory' (Page 5), 'Executive Order 91' (Pages 17-18), and 'GREAT Grant Program' (Page 24).
checks.b_evidence_location: Entire document, between Section 4 and Section 13, and between Section 15 and Section 25.

checks.a_analysis: The tables present in the output (Broadband Speeds and Typical Broadband Usage) contain all the data rows found in the corresponding source pages (3 and 4). No data loss within these tables was observed.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: While many pages are missing entirely (C3), the sections that were successfully converted (e.g., Broadband Technology Types on Page 2) do not show internal text omissions or truncation. The content within the converted sections matches the source closely.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The converted text accurately captures technical details such as speed ranges (1.5 Mbps to 80 Mbps for DSL) and threshold values (1-2 Gbps for DOCSIS 3.1) without altering the meaning. OCR errors were negligible.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed each section of the JSON output against the provided source PDF. Each text element, including slide titles, list items, and table data, appears exactly as many times in the output as it does in the source document. There is no evidence of repeated paragraphs, sections, or duplicated content within tables or lists.

The JSON uses the slide titles (e.g., 'What is Broadband?', 'Broadband Technology Types', 'Broadband Speeds') as the primary section headers. This accurately reflects the flat structural hierarchy of a slide presentation where each slide represents a top-level section. No heading levels are skipped, and the hierarchy logic is consistent throughout the document.

The tables on page 3 ('Broadband Speeds') and page 4 ('Typical Broadband Usage') are correctly structured. On page 3, category rows like 'General Usage' are properly handled using colspans to span the width of the table. On page 4, the 4x5 usage matrix is accurately mapped with four columns, including the empty corner cell. Other layout elements (pages 13, 27) use tables to preserve side-by-side visual orientation, which is a valid structural interpretation.

The reading order within each section and across sections matches the logical flow of the original presentation. Text blocks, tables, and images are sequenced as they appear visually (top-to-bottom, left-to-right).

The multi-level list on page 2 ('Broadband Technology Types') is correctly converted into an unordered list with nested lists for sub-bullets. The relationship between the technology name (e.g., DSL) and its descriptive sub-points is perfectly preserved.

Hyperlinks provided in the text (such as the FCC Broadband Speed and Household Broadband guides) are correctly captured with their respective URLs. Contact links, specifically the email address on page 26, are properly identified and prefixed with 'mailto:'.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the overall visual organization of the source PDF. The sequence of headings, text blocks, and tables follows the original document's flow. While graphical elements like the hexagonal containers on pages 10 and 12 or the chevron shapes on page 23 have been simplified to plain text blocks, the information remains in the correct relative position and the hierarchy is preserved. The document is presented in a standard single-column responsive layout which is expected for HTML conversion.

The tables in the document are rendered correctly. The 'Broadband Speeds' table (source page 3) and the 'Typical Broadband Usage' table (source page 4) are converted into functional HTML tables where rows and columns are clearly aligned, and headers are visually distinct. The data remains easily readable and understandable for a sighted user.

Multiple meaningful images, maps, and charts from the source PDF are missing in the HTML output. Instead of the actual visual content, large green placeholder boxes are rendered. This includes the title page image collage (page 1), critical data visualizations such as the 'North Carolina Broadband Service Inventory' map (page 5), the adoption rate map (page 11), the series of house graphics illustrating adoption (pages 6-9), and the 'Technical Assistance Regions' map (page 19). These are not decorative elements but central pieces of information in the presentation.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains several critical images including a North Carolina map titled 'North Carolina Broadband Service Inventory' (page 5), another map titled '2017 County Broadband Adoption Rates' (page 11), and a regional map on page 19.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output displays green placeholder boxes instead of these images. For example, under the heading 'North Carolina Broadband Service Inventory', there is only a large green rectangle. Similar green boxes appear in place of maps and graphics throughout the document.

checks.a_analysis: No visual duplication of content blocks was observed in the HTML output. Each section, list, and table corresponds to a single instance in the source PDF.
